rulings.Case Title: FELIX DE ANDA VS. CALAVERAS ASBESTOS, LTD et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JUNE 25, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Calaveras Asbestos, Ltd.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s claim for punitive damages is denied as improperly set within 30 days of trial in violation of C.C.P. 437c(a). Defendant set its motion within 30 days of trial without an order from the court allowing it to do so. Plaintiff¿s objection to defendant¿s improperly set motion is sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT GOETZ VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED

rulings.Case Title: LARRELL FORTNER et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JUNE 25, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant Collins Electrical Company Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Even if defendant sustained its initial burden, Dr. Schwartz¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Fortner¿s colon cancer was caused by his exposure to asbestos. (Garrett v. Howmedica Osteonics Corp. (2013) 214 Cal.App.4th 173.) The information and explanation set forth in Dr. Schwartz¿s declaration were sufficient to support his opinions for purposes of opposing this summary judgment motion. (Id. at 187.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LARRELL FORTNER et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, JUNE 25, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 through 5 and granted as to issue 6. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Fortner was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Fortner¿s deposition testimony and plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to punitive damages, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
